session_summary: Created 6 combat VFX scripts, 3 prefabs, wired all enemy/attack data assets and Player prefab
done: EnemyDeathVFX: radial burst particles on enemy kill
DashTrailVFX: particle trail during dash (on Player prefab)
PlayerHurtVFX: sprite flash + hit particles + screen flash on damage (on Player prefab)
SkillHitVFX: generic impact burst for skill/melee hits
EnemySpawnVFX: upward materialization burst on spawn
KnockbackVFX: directional cone burst with static Spawn() API, integrated into AttackHitbox and DamageSkillEffect
Created 3 standalone VFX prefabs in Prefabs/Effects/
Added DashTrailVFX and PlayerHurtVFX components to Player.prefab
Wired EnemyDeathVFX and EnemySpawnVFX to all 8 EnemyData assets
Wired SkillHitVFX to all 11 player AttackData and 7 enemy EnemyAttackData assets
decisions: Used runtime particle system creation (not prefab particle systems) to match existing PowerUpVFX pattern
KnockbackVFX uses static Spawn() API instead of prefab — already auto-integrated into combat code
All VFX use URP Particles/Unlit shader with Sprites/Default fallback
Kept particle counts conservative for performance (8-20 per burst)
next_steps: Playtest VFX in-editor to verify visuals and performance
Tune particle colors per-enemy if needed (EnemyDeathVFX is tintable via Inspector)
Consider adding DynamicFogSystem implementation (currently empty placeholder)
Add VFX for status effects/buffs/debuffs when buff system matures
